output_formatting_guidelines.overview: Output must be plain text suitable for Jenkins console viewing. No ANSI escape codes, no markdown tables, no fancy formatting. The goal is clarity and readability in a terminal environment.

rule.name: no_ansi_codes
rule.description: Never use ANSI color codes or terminal escape sequences

rule.name: no_markdown_tables
rule.description: Do not use markdown table syntax
rule.use_instead: Simple text with clear spacing and indentation

rule.name: plain_text_emphasis
rule.description: Use text-based emphasis instead of formatting codes

techniques.technique: UPPERCASE for risk levels (HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW)

rule.name: line_length
rule.description: Keep lines reasonably short for console readability
rule.guideline: Aim for 80-100 characters per line maximum
rule.approach: Break long descriptions into multiple lines with proper indentation

section.name: header
section.purpose: Clear visual separator at the start

section.name: summary
section.format: SUMMARY: Brief overview of what changed (1-3 sentences total).

guidelines.guideline: Start with "SUMMARY:" on its own line
Mention number of files if relevant

section.example: SUMMARY: Modified order processing logic in 3 files. Added null checks and updated error handling. No database schema changes.

section.name: risk
section.format:
RISK ASSESSMENT:
File: path/to/file.java Change: Brief description of what changed (1-2 sentences) Risk: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW
File: path/to/another.java Change: Brief description Risk: MEDIUM

guidelines.guideline: Each file entry separated by blank line
Use 2-space indentation for Change and Risk
Order by risk level (HIGH first, then MEDIUM, then LOW)

section.example:
RISK ASSESSMENT:
File: src/main/java/com/example/OrderService.java Change: Removed null check before calling customer.getEmail(). Now directly accesses email field without validation. Risk: HIGH
File: src/main/java/com/example/OrderController.java Change: Added new endpoint /api/orders/bulk without rate limiting. Risk: MEDIUM
File: src/test/java/com/example/OrderServiceTest.java Change: Added unit tests for new validation logic. Risk: LOW

section.name: watch_for
section.format: WATCH FOR: - Specific concern 1 - Specific concern 2 - Specific concern 3

guidelines.guideline: Each concern on its own line
Be specific - reference actual code locations when possible

section.example: WATCH FOR: - Null-safety: OrderService.processOrder() now dereferences customer without check - Concurrency: Shared orderCache modified without synchronization in OrderService - Missing tests: New bulk endpoint has no integration tests - Performance: New query in loop at OrderRepository.findByStatus() (line 45)

section.name: footer
section.purpose: Clear visual separator at the end

case.name: single_file_change
case.approach: Still use full structure, just with one file entry

case.name: many_files
case.approach: Group similar low-risk changes: "Files: test/*.java (5 files) Change: Added unit tests for new features Risk: LOW"

complete_example.scenario: Order service changes with mixed risk levels
complete_example.output:
======================================== GIT DIFF OVERVIEW ========================================
SUMMARY: Modified order processing in 4 files. Added new bulk order endpoint, updated validation logic, and added corresponding tests. One high-risk null-safety issue.
RISK ASSESSMENT:
File: src/main/java/com/example/orders/service/OrderService.java Change: Removed null check before customer.getEmail() call in processOrder(). Now directly accesses email without validation. Risk: HIGH
File: src/main/java/com/example/orders/controller/OrderController.java Change: Added new POST /api/orders/bulk endpoint for batch processing. No rate limiting or size validation on batch. Risk: MEDIUM
File: src/main/java/com/example/orders/model/Order.java Change: Added new status field with validation. Updated equals/hashCode. Risk: LOW
File: src/test/java/com/example/orders/service/OrderServiceTest.java Change: Added 3 new test cases for validation logic. Risk: LOW
WATCH FOR: - Null-safety: OrderService.processOrder() dereferences customer.getEmail() without check (line 87) - Security surface: New bulk endpoint accepts unbounded array size - Missing tests: Bulk endpoint has no integration tests - Behavior change: Order status validation now rejects "PENDING_REVIEW" state
========================================

anti_pattern.name: verbose_descriptions
anti_pattern.bad: File: src/main/java/com/example/OrderService.java Change: This file contains the order service implementation which handles all order processing logic including validation, persistence, and notification. In this change, we modified the processOrder method to add additional validation for customer email addresses...
anti_pattern.good: File: src/main/java/com/example/OrderService.java Change: Added email validation in processOrder(). Now checks format before sending notifications.

anti_pattern.name: using_markdown
anti_pattern.bad:
**RISK ASSESSMENT:**
| File | Risk | Description | |------|------|-------------| | OrderService.java | HIGH | Removed null check |
anti_pattern.good:
RISK ASSESSMENT:
File: OrderService.java Change: Removed null check before dereferencing customer object. Risk: HIGH

anti_pattern.name: unclear_risk_levels
anti_pattern.bad: Risk: high-ish Risk: probably medium Risk: not too bad
anti_pattern.good: Risk: HIGH Risk: MEDIUM Risk: LOW
